California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
May 7, 199 0
Robert R. wellington
city Attorney
City of Marina
505 Abrego Street
l,lonterey, CA 93940
Re: H:'"li""T:' I53:Hl 
Assistance
Dear ur. wellington:
This is in response to your letter requestingr advice regard-
ing application of the confl ict-of-interest provisions of the
Politicat Reform Act (the 'Act") 1/ to the uaiina City Council.
Since your question is general in nature and you have not narned a
specific elected official 
on whose behalf you have requested this
advice, we can only provide the following j.nformal response to
your question pt..ir.ii 
t- Regulation 183i9(c) lcopy en-closed,).2|
OUESTION
Does the public Aenerally exception applicable to small
jurisdictions apply to the city of tilarina, where the city
boundaries include 30,113 persons, but the city's population
independent of the federal areas, is only L6,'733?
CONCLUSTON
Regulation 18703.1 would not be available
Council of llarina because the jurisdiction of
the entire city, including the population near
been annexed to the city.
for the city
the city council is
Fort ord that has
l/ 
covernment code Sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory refer-
ences are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Com-
mission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section 18ooo, et seq. A11 references to regn:lations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
2/ Infonnal assj.stance does not provide the requestor with the
irnrnunity provided by an opini.on or formal written advice. (Section
83114; Regulation 18329 (c) (3) . )
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916)322-5660


Our File No. I-90-249
Page 2
FACTS
The city of l,tarina is situated west of the United States Army
base at Fort ord. Several years ago, Marina annexed a portion of
Ford Ord, adding 13,380 persons to the city's existing population
of 16,733.
The city of Marina does not provide municipal services to the
area, such as police or fire protectj,on, and does not collect
property taxes fron the area. In addition, the area is not
subject to the city's transient occupancy tax, property transfer
tax, planning and land use/building inspection fees and the sales
and use tax on retail sales. Ilowever, the city does receive tax
benefits based on population due to the inclusion of the area.
Further, the residents of that area do vote in city elections.
Because the city covers a smaII geographic area, it is not
unusual to have nore than one councilnember disqualified due to
conflicts of interest. 
You have asked whether the public gener-
ally exception applicable to snaIl jurisdictions, Regulation
18703.1, appLies to the city of Marina, where the city boundaries
include 30,113 persons, but the city's population independent of
the federaL areas is only 15,733.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official 
from making,
participating in naking, or othersise using his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which the official 
has a
financial interest. 
Section 87103 specifies that an official 
has
a financial interest within the meanj.ng of section 87100 if it is
reasonably foreseeable that the decision wiLl have a material
financiaL effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public
generally, on the official or a member of his or her irnnediate
fanily or on any real property in which the public official 
has a
direct or indirect interest lrorth S1,ooo or more. (Section
87103 (b) . )
Regnrlation 18703.1 provides that under certain specified
circumstances the effect of a governnental decision on a public
official's 
principal residence is not distinguishable from the
effect on the public Aenerally in suall jurisdictions. 
Subdivi-
sion (a) of the regnrlation provides that the exception is only
available if:
The public official's 
agency has jurisdiction
over a population of 25, OOO or less, coverin.r a
geographic area of ten square miles or 1ess.


Our File No. I-9 o-24 9
Page 3
You have asked how subsection (a) of Regulation La7o3.13 I
. (copy enclosed) is interpreted with respect to the term ttjurisdic-
tion.rr Section 82035 of the Act defines "jurisdictionrr as:
...the state rrith respect to a state agency and,
with respect to a }oca], government agency, the
reqion. countv, citv. district or other
qeoqraphical area in which it has 'iurisdiction.
The jurisdiction of a menber of a regional coastal
zone conservation conmission shall be the pennit
area in erhich the regj.onal conmission has jurisdic-
tion. 
Real property shall be deemed to be "within
the jurisdictiontr wj-th respect to a 1oca1 govern-
nent agency if the property or any part of it is
located within or not nore than two rniles outside
the boundaries of the jurisdiction or within two
niles of any land owned or used by the loca1
government agency.
Funk and wagnalls standard college Dictionary (1974) defines
rr jurisdictionti as the lawful trright to exercise official 
author-
ity, whether execut j.ve, legislative or judicial. " or, in the
alternative the trterritory within... [which] such ]at fu1 authority
may be lar^rfully exercised. " Under your facts, while the city
council's authority with respect to the area in question is
linited in conparison with the council's authority over the rest
of the city, the council, nevertheless, has sone authority and
official duties with respect to the regi.on. This is further sup-
ported by the fact that the persons residing j.n that area vote in
city elections.
zurther, it lrou1d appear that not only are the persons resid-
ing in that area constituents of the city council, but they nay
also be iDpacted by city council decisions. In 1985 in In re
Leqan (9 FPPC ops. 1, copy enclosed) the Connissj-on stated:
The rrpubLicrr includes the entire jurisdiction
for the agency in guestion, particularly where an
elective body is being considered, because a1f of
the County's residents are constituents of the
various members of the Board of Supervisors and
rnany reside in the various incorporated cities
within the County. They are all inpacted in one
rray or another by the County,s land-use decisions,
even if not directly subject to the Countyrs land-
use jurisdiction. 
We see no reason to alter our
Jt 
Regarlation 18703.1 was adopted by the conmission in Decernber
1989. The regrulation will be subnitted to the Office of
Adninistrative Law in the near future. 
Horrever, in the interiro,
the Conmission will be advising on the basis of the language of
the new regulation.


Our Fi■e No. ■-90-249
Page 4
interpretation that the residents, and businesses
and property owners of the County of Santa Clara
are the trpublic" for purposes of decisions being
made by the Board of Supervisors.
In re Leqanr .9.9E8. r at 1,2.
Thus, persons in cities within a county are part of the
jurisdiction of the county board of supervj,sors despite the fact
that the cities are not directly subject to the board of
supervisor's authority. 
we believe this rationale aPplies here as
wel].
Fina11y, the clear intent of the regulation in guestion was
to consider the jurisdiction of a city council, to be the city
boundaries for purposes of subdivision (a). In the nenorandurn in
support of the regulation (copy enclosed) we said:
Subdivision (a) offers tlro aLternatives to
specify the size of the jurisdiction for Purposes
of the exception:
-- a population ranging in size of less than
15. !-0-9-L9-25.,-9P9.; and
-- a geographic area ranging in size of less
than five to fifteen square niles.
According to the League of California cities,
there are approxiBately 143 cities rrith populations
of 15,ooo or less covering an area of five square
Diles or less. Another 35 cities cover an area of
fifteen square uiles or less, vith poPulations of
15,000 or less. Since there are a total of 453
cities in california, those with a population of
15,ooo or less covering 15 square niles or less
conprise approxiuately 40t of aII cities in
california. 
we have no infornation on other agen-
cies with surall jurisdictions, such as special
districts, 
school districts or other sinilar public
entities.
No special use of the tern tr jurisdictionrr was suggested in
the menorandun, nor does it appear that the information provided
by the League of Cities deternined the population of city other
than by considering the city boundaries. Thus, it appears from
this langruage that the term tr jurisdictionrr lras meant to reflect
the population in the cj,ties' boundaries. Moreover, it is a
fundanental rule of statutory construction that an exception to a
general rule is to be narroirly construed. (Julius coldtnan's E('q
Citv v. Air Pollution control Dept. of ventura Countv (1981) 116
Ca1.App. 3d 74L,. Valdez v. Federal lr{ut. fns. Co. (7969) 272
Cal.App. 2d 223.) Regnrlation 18703.1 is an exception to the


Our FiIe No. I-9 O-2 4 9
Page 5
general rules requj,ring disqualification on the basis of an
official's 
economic interests. 
As such, the interpretation of the
reg'ulation which confines application of the exception is
preferred. Accordingl,y, the entire population of the city,
including persons residing at Fort ord, is the relevant population
for purposes of determining whether Regulation 18703.1 applies.
Consequently, ue conclude that the jurisdiction of Marina is
the entire city, including the population near Fort ord that was
annexed to the city. 
Thus, Regutation 18703.1 would not be avail-
able for the City Council of Marina. r/
If you have any further guestions regarding this natter,
please feel free to contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
C/\-\_
By:
John W. wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
KED: JWW: plh
Enclosures
4/ Absent facts to the contrary, we would also conclude that the
city,s planning conmission has jurisdiction that is coterminous
with the city council. Consequently, the exception would
similarly not be available for the Marina planning Conrnission.
